content.text:
The claim is that content density is defined as "the ratio of information visible compared to the space available," and that two discrete modes exist: "comfortable" (default) and "compact" (for data-intensive views).

Let me check the source and the supporting quote carefully.

The supporting quote states: "Content density is defined by the ratio of information visible compared to the space available in the interface. Comfortable is the standard density level of the system, active by default. Compact is an additional density level for data intensive views."

The claim under review says: "two discrete modes are supported: 'comfortable' (default) and 'compact' (for data-intensive views)."

This is a direct restatement of the quote. Let me verify the source quality and check for any contradictions.
